body:
## Context

The payment flow (#180), event charges (#161), shares (#152), and membership tracking all create moments where members need to know something happened. Without notifications, members have to log into the portal and check — nobody will do that. These emails are what make the system feel alive.

All emails go through Resend (free tier: 3,000/month, already integrated) using React Email templates (pattern exists from newsletter).

## What to do

### 1. Email templates

Create React Email templates in `src/emails/`:

**Payment lifecycle:**
| Template | Trigger | Subject |
|----------|---------|---------|
| `PaymentConfirmed` | Admin confirms pending payment (#180) | "Your $75 payment was confirmed" |
| `PaymentRejected` | Admin rejects pending payment (#180) | "Your payment couldn't be matched" |
| `EventChargeAssigned` | Admin assigns event cost (#161) | "You've been charged $35 for Family Night" |
| `SharesPurchased` | Member buys shares (#152) | "3 shares purchased — pending payment" |
| `SharesPaid` | Admin marks shares paid (#152) | "Your 3 shares ($150) have been confirmed" |

**Membership lifecycle:**
| Template | Trigger | Subject |
|----------|---------|---------|
| `DuesReminder` | Cron — 14 days + 3 days before expiry | "Your membership expires in 14 days" |
| `MembershipExpired` | Cron — day after expiry | "Your membership expired on April 30" |
| `MembershipRenewed` | Payment confirmed with type=membership | "Membership extended through May 2027" |

**Account lifecycle:**
| Template | Trigger | Subject |
|----------|---------|---------|
| `WelcomeMember` | User completes invite + sets password (#179) | "Welcome to St. Basil's" |
| `FamilyLinked` | Admin links profile to family | "You've been linked to the Nijo Family" |

### 2. Send emails from existing server actions

No new services — just add `await resend.emails.send()` at the end of existing actions:

- `confirmPayment()` → `PaymentConfirmed`
- `rejectPayment()` → `PaymentRejected`
- `assignEventCosts()` → `EventChargeAssigned` (one email per family)
- `buyShares()` → `SharesPurchased`
- `markSharesPaid()` → `SharesPaid`
- `recordPaymentReceived()` where type=membership → `MembershipRenewed`

### 3. Dues reminder cron job

Daily cron (Vercel Cron, free on hobby plan):

```
/api/cron/dues-reminders
```

- Query families where `membership_expires_at` = today + 14 days → send 14-day reminder
- Query families where `membership_expires_at` = today + 3 days → send 3-day reminder
- Query families where `membership_expires_at` = yesterday → send expired notice
- Protected by `CRON_SECRET` header (Vercel injects this automatically)

### 4. Notification preferences

Add to `profiles`:
```sql
ALTER TABLE profiles ADD COLUMN notification_preferences JSONB
  DEFAULT '{"payments": true, "membership": true, "shares": true, "events": true}';
```

- Every email checks the relevant preference before sending
- Member can toggle in portal: Settings page or a "Manage notifications" link in email footer
- Every email includes an unsubscribe/manage link

### 5. Email footer

All transactional emails include:
- Church name + address
- "Manage notification preferences" link → `/member/settings`
- "View in portal" link → deep link to relevant tab

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] Payment confirmed/rejected emails send when admin acts on pending payment
- [ ] Event charge email sends to each affected family
- [ ] Share purchase and payment confirmation emails work
- [ ] Dues reminders send at 14 days, 3 days, and expiry
- [ ] Membership renewal email sends when dues payment confirmed
- [ ] Welcome email sends after invite completion
- [ ] All emails respect notification preferences
- [ ] Every email has manage preferences + portal link in footer
- [ ] Cron job runs daily, protected by CRON_SECRET

## Free tier impact

Estimated volume: ~300 emails/month for 100-150 families. Well under Resend's 3,000/month free tier. Vercel Cron is free on hobby plan (once/day minimum interval is fine for dues reminders).

## Dependencies

- #180 (payment flow — confirm/reject triggers)
- #152 (shares actions — purchase/paid triggers)
- #154 (admin payment actions — charge assignment trigger)
- #161 (event cost assignment trigger)
- #179 (auth callback — welcome email trigger)
- Existing Resend + React Email infrastructure

## Suggested sub-tickets

1. React Email templates (all 10)
2. Wire payment/shares/charge emails into existing server actions
3. Dues reminder cron job (`/api/cron/dues-reminders`)
4. Notification preferences column + settings UI
5. Welcome + family linked emails
title: Transactional notification emails for payments, membership, and account events

body:
## Context

The zero-fee payment flow (#180) handles Zelle/Venmo/Cash App with manual treasurer confirmation. But some members want to pay online with a button click — no switching to their banking app, no copying reference memos. Every.org makes this possible at zero cost for ACH and near-zero for cards.

This is the "Pay Online" option alongside the existing P2P methods.

## ⚠️ CHECK WITH TREASURER FIRST

**Every.org acts as a fiscal intermediary.** Donations flow through Every.org's 501(c)(3) and are disbursed to St. Basil's. This means:

- **Tax receipts come from Every.org**, not from St. Basil's directly
- Receipts say "via Every.org for St. Basil's Syriac Orthodox Church"
- Every.org handles IRS compliance for the donation
- Disbursement timing may vary (not instant like Zelle)

**Before implementing, confirm with the treasurer that this is acceptable.** Some churches require tax receipts to come directly from their own 501(c)(3). If this is a blocker, Givebutter (which also has an API and $0 fees with donor tips) is the fallback — see the payment integrations mockup for comparison.

## What Every.org offers

| Method | Fee to church | Fee to donor | Confirmation |
|--------|--------------|-------------|-------------|
| ACH (bank transfer) | **$0** | $0 | Auto via webhook |
| Credit/debit card | **2.2% + $0.30** | $0 | Auto via webhook |
| Stock/DAF | **$0** | $0 | Auto via webhook |

No tip prompts. No platform fee. No monthly fee. Donor pays exactly what they see.

## What to do

### 1. Register St. Basil's on Every.org

- Church must be a registered 501(c)(3) — Every.org verifies via EIN
- Create org profile at every.org
- Get API credentials for webhook integration

### 2. Add Every.org donate widget

Package: `@every.org/donate-button` (open-source, npm)

Embed in the payment flow (#180) as the fourth option:

```
Choose payment method:

[Zelle]  [Venmo]  [Cash App]  [Pay Online]
                                    │
                          Every.org widget
                          ACH = free
                          Card = 2.2% + $0.30
```

Widget is customizable — match St. Basil's colors, set default amount, lock to specific frequency (monthly for dues).

### 3. Webhook integration

Every.org fires webhooks on successful donations:

```
POST /api/webhooks/everyorg
```

- Verify webhook signature
- Parse donation: amount, donor email, frequency, method
- Match donor email to `profiles.email` → get `family_id`
- Create `payments` row with `status = 'confirmed'`, `method = 'online'`
- Fire downstream side effects (extend membership, mark share paid, etc.)
- No manual treasurer confirmation needed — payment is verified by Every.org

### 4. Guide donors toward ACH

ACH is free. Cards cost 2.2% + $0.30. Default the widget to ACH and show the fee difference clearly:

- ACH option: green "Free!" badge
- Card option: amber "$1.95 fee" badge
- Most regular donors (monthly dues) will choose ACH once they see it's free

### 5. Recurring payments

Every.org supports monthly/annual recurring donations. For membership dues:
- Member sets up recurring ACH on Every.org
- Webhook fires each month → auto-creates payment + extends membership
- No manual intervention, no treasurer work, $0 fees

## Design reference

Interactive comparison mockup showing all payment integrations side by side: [mockup-payment-integrations.html](https://gist.github.com/georgenijo/TBD)

See the "Every.org" tab for the ACH/card toggle UI, and the "Compare" tab for side-by-side cost breakdown across all options.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] Treasurer confirms Every.org fiscal intermediary model is acceptable
- [ ] St. Basil's registered on Every.org with verified 501(c)(3)
- [ ] Every.org widget embedded in payment flow as "Pay Online" option
- [ ] ACH shown as default with "Free" badge, card shown with fee amount
- [ ] Webhook receives donation events and auto-creates confirmed payment rows
- [ ] Donor email matched to profile for family linking
- [ ] Recurring donations supported (monthly/annual)
- [ ] Payment appears in member portal immediately after webhook fires

## Resource impact

- **Every.org**: free, no usage limits
- **Supabase**: one webhook endpoint, negligible load
- **Vercel**: one API route for webhook, negligible
- **No new monthly costs**

## Dependencies

- #180 (zero-fee payment flow — Every.org is the "Pay Online" tab)
- #148 (payments table)
- #184 (transactional notifications — send confirmation email after webhook)

## Relationship to #180

This extends #180 by adding a fourth payment method. The Zelle/Venmo/Cash App flow with manual confirmation stays as-is. Every.org adds the automated online option. Both coexist in the same payment UI.
title: Integrate Every.org for free online payments (ACH + card)

body:
## Problem

The admin panel has two separate sections — **Subscribers** and **Users** — that can be confusing:

- **Subscribers** are newsletter-only email signups (stored in `subscribers` table)
- **Users** are authenticated accounts with roles (stored in `profiles` / Supabase auth)

There's no clear public-facing flow for how someone becomes a subscriber vs a user. Questions:

1. How does a visitor subscribe to the newsletter? (The old PHP form is broken, and there's no current signup widget on the public site)
2. Users are currently invite-only via admin. Should members be able to self-register?
3. Should subscribing to the newsletter automatically happen when a user is invited/created?
4. Should the admin UI make the relationship between subscribers and users clearer?

## Suggestion

Consider one or more of:
- Add a newsletter signup form to the public site (replace broken PHP form)
- Auto-subscribe users to the newsletter when they're invited (with opt-out)
- Show a link between subscriber emails and user accounts in the admin UI when they match
- Add a note/tooltip in the Subscribers page explaining what subscribers are vs users

## Screenshots

Subscribers page shows 3 newsletter signups:
![Subscribers](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/placeholder-subscribers)

Users page shows 1 authenticated admin:
![Users](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/placeholder-users)

## Context

Discovered during manual testing after pipeline batch shipped 21 issues. The two features were built by separate issues and the relationship between them was never explicitly defined.
title: Clarify subscriber vs user signup flow and consolidation
